weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, light rain. Sunday: 11°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney looks like a blast! Despite the chance of rain on Saturday, there are plenty of indoor and outdoor activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from free museum adventures to Easter-themed events and creative play spaces. Get ready for a weekend filled with fun, exploration, and maybe even a chocolate treat or two!

activities.title: Easter Fun for the Whole Family
activities.description: A free Easter family event with crafts, face painting, photo booth, classic games, chill-out zone, and children's entertainment.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of engaging activities suitable for a 3-year-old, including creative crafts and entertaining games. The chill-out zone provides a space for relaxation if needed.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Woodford Station, then a short bus ride or walk to High Elms.
transport.tip: Check the bus schedule in advance to minimize travel time.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: A free library club with a reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a calm and engaging environment for young children to explore books and develop a love for reading. The reading corner is perfect for quiet time.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses run to Hackney Central, check local routes for the Hackney Showroom
transport.tip: Can combine with a trip to Hackney Central

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds and a Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling, crafts, and singalongs.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging play spaces designed for young children to explore stories and use their imaginations. The variety of activities caters to different interests.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk to the centre.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific baby and toddler sessions.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries focused on design, imagine, and play. Includes a Mini Museum for under 2s and various classes like Sound Explorers and Mini Play.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for children, with hands-on exhibits and activities that encourage creativity and exploration. The Mini Museum caters to the sensory needs of younger toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for specific class schedules.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with farm animals, a cafe, and fresh vegetables.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an opportunity for children to interact with animals and learn about farming in an urban setting. Perfect for a hands-on experience.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses serve the area, check local routes.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme, located in the Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging outdoor play space for toddlers with a unique theme. The pond theme can spark curiosity and imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk into the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to other attractions in the Olympic Park.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover illustrated animals hidden among the blooming habitats, identify letters, and complete a secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun and educational activity based on a beloved children's book. Encourages observation and problem-solving skills.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Walthamstow Central, then a short walk to the Wetlands.
transport.tip: Bring comfortable shoes for walking.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Library club with a reading corner and book-pairing service
activities.whyItWorks: Has diverse books and a reading corner

transport.fromHackney: Bus
transport.tip: Check bus routes

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo, each revealing a secret letter to uncover a hidden code.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the excitement of an egg hunt with the opportunity to see a variety of animals. The animal-themed challenges make learning fun and engaging.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston. A short bus ride or walk will take you to the zoo.
transport.tip: Book zoo tickets online in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur exhibits, interactive displays, and seasonal activities. Easter-themed workshops and storytelling sessions may be available.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a combination of education and entertainment with fascinating exhibits that appeal to children of all ages. The dinosaur exhibits are particularly popular.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits on space travel, technology, and science experiments. The Garden is an interactive space for younger children.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a hands-on learning experience for children with exhibits that encourage exploration and discovery. The Garden is specifically designed for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for special events and demonstrations.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Family Fun
activities.description: Meet characters from the ship's past, follow a family trail, and enjoy musical storytelling sessions inspired by the crew.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers an immersive experience that brings history to life for children. The characters and storytelling sessions are engaging and entertaining.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Canada Water, then the Jubilee Line to North Greenwich. Transfer to local buses or walk to the Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Check the website for the schedule of character appearances and storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace - Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk. This is an outdoor energy-burner.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early in the day – the grounds are calmer before lunch.

activities.title: Battersea Park Children's Zoo
activities.description: Zoo with wide array of animals
activities.whyItWorks: The zoo features a wide array of animals.

transport.fromHackney: Train and Bus
transport.tip: Can explore the park as well

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Learn about London's waterfront history with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, it is not overly crowded

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Check tube routes

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families, including shows, musical adventures, puppetry, and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a diverse range of activities suitable for different ages and interests. The mix of free and paid events provides flexibility.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre's website for the full schedule and booking information.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: The world's most beloved bear comes to the stage in a heartwarming musical adaptation.
activities.whyItWorks: A charming and family-friendly musical that brings a beloved character to life. The engaging storyline and catchy songs will entertain both children and adults.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend performances.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: The performance is engaging and the play is great for creativity

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Check the website

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages creativity and imaginitive play

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Check website

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A magical show
activities.whyItWorks: Family friendly

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Check tube schedule

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Heartwarming and hilarious adventure
activities.whyItWorks: Is a show

transport.fromHackney: Bus
transport.tip: Check bus route

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Hunt the Giant Easter Egg Trail, Meet the Easter Bunny, Indoor Playbarn, Easter Entertainment with the Hobblers and Zoo and Farm Animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Has lots of different things to do

transport.fromHackney: Check Transport
transport.tip: Check Website
